meta.note: The plot advisor reads this with the data profile (from profile_data.mjs) to recommend the best-fit chart. Bias toward expressive, structure-revealing encodings over boring defaults. Always name a pick + a runner-up, justify in one line.

charts.name: Beeswarm
charts.dataShape: one quantitative, one categorical (small n per group)
charts.why: Shows every observation and the shape of each group — honest about n and spread, unlike a bar of means.

charts.name: Ridgeline
charts.dataShape: one quantitative across many ordered categories
charts.why: Stacked densities reveal shifts in shape across groups in little space.
charts.whenNot: Few groups or you need exact values.

charts.name: Violin + jittered points
charts.why: Density + raw points: distribution shape without hiding the data.

charts.name: Slope chart
charts.dataShape: one quantitative at two timepoints/conditions, paired
charts.why: Each subject is a line; direction and crossings read instantly — far better than grouped bars.

charts.name: Dumbbell / connected-dot
charts.dataShape: two values per category (e.g. before/after, A/B)
charts.why: The gap IS the message; sortable by gap size.

charts.name: Hexbin density
charts.why: Reveals structure where a scatter would be an overplotted blob.

charts.name: 2D contour density
charts.why: Smooth density contours expose modes/clusters and overlap between groups.

charts.name: Scatter + regression + CI band
charts.why: Direct relationship with fitted trend and uncertainty band.

charts.name: Bland–Altman
charts.dataShape: two quantitative measuring the same thing
charts.why: House default for method/agreement: bias + limits of agreement vs mean.
charts.whenNot: Not comparing two methods of one quantity.

charts.name: Lollipop
charts.why: Same info as a bar with far less ink; cleaner at many categories.

charts.name: Small multiples (faceted lines)
charts.dataShape: quantitative over time across many groups
charts.why: One trend per panel — comparable, uncluttered, no spaghetti.

charts.name: Bump / rank-over-time
charts.why: Tracks position changes clearly across timepoints.

charts.name: Arc diagram
charts.why: Linear node order + arcs keeps a small network readable.

charts.name: Chord diagram
charts.why: Shows reciprocal flows and dominant pairings compactly.
charts.whenNot: Many categories with tiny flows (use a heatmap).

charts.name: Clustered heatmap
charts.why: Row/column clustering surfaces block structure in the data.

charts.name: Kaplan–Meier + risk table
charts.why: Standard for survival; step curves + at-risk table + CI bands.

charts.name: Forest plot
charts.dataShape: effect sizes with CIs across studies/subgroups
charts.why: Point estimates + CIs aligned for instant comparison.
